assertion_results.text: The plan determines feature-branch workflow mode (not direct-to-main) based on the atomicity constraints in the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md states 'Selected mode: feature-branch' with a detailed rationale citing two atomicity indicators: (1) 'Coordinated schema migration (indicator 1)' explaining that merging migration alone or code alone would break the build, and (2) 'All-or-nothing delivery requirement (indicator 3 -- cross-cutting refactor)' citing the feature's NFRs that 'all changes must land together.' These directly reference atomicity constraints from the feature description's Non-Functional Requirements section.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type create-branch is generated as the first task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-1-create-feature-branch.md is the first task file and contains '## Bookend Type\ncreate-branch'. Its title is 'Task 1 -- Create feature branch TC-9005 from main'.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type merge-branch is generated as the last task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-8-merge-feature-branch.md is the last task file (task 8 of 8) and contains '## Bookend Type\nmerge-branch'. Its title is 'Task 8 -- Merge feature branch TC-9005 to main'.

assertion_results.text: All intermediate tasks (between the create-branch and merge-branch bookend tasks) have Target Branch set to TC-9005
assertion_results.evidence: Tasks 2-7 all have '## Target Branch\nTC-9005': task-2-migration-advisory-status-enum.md line 8, task-3-update-entity-definitions.md line 8, task-4-update-advisory-service.md line 8, task-5-update-advisory-endpoints.md line 8, task-6-update-ingestion-pipeline.md line 8, task-7-update-integration-tests.md line 8.

assertion_results.text: The create-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
The merge-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
All intermediate tasks list the create-branch bookend task as a dependency
assertion_results.evidence: All six intermediate tasks (2-7) list 'Depends on: Task 1 -- Create feature branch TC-9005 from main' in their Dependencies section: task-2 line 49, task-3 line 62, task-4 line 47, task-5 line 45, task-6 line 52, task-7 line 44.

assertion_results.text: The merge-branch bookend task lists all intermediate tasks as dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: task-8-merge-feature-branch.md Dependencies section (lines 23-29) lists all six intermediate tasks: Task 2 (migration), Task 3 (entity definitions), Task 4 (advisory service), Task 5 (advisory endpoints), Task 6 (ingestion pipeline), Task 7 (integration tests).

assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 (task-1-create-feature-branch.md) is missing: Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes sections. It only has: Repository, Target Branch, Bookend Type, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies. Task 8 (task-8-merge-feature-branch.md) is also missing: Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes sections. It only has: Repository, Target Branch, Bookend Type, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies. Intermediate tasks 2-7 all have the required sections.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes a workflow:feature-branch label decision to be applied to the feature issue
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 29 states: 'The `workflow:feature-branch` label will be applied to the feature issue TC-9005.'

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: No output file contains any reference to 'sha256', 'digest', or '[sdlc-workflow]'. Grep across all output files for 'digest', 'sha256', and 'comment' returned no matches for digest-related content. No description digest comments are present in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No output file contains any reference to 'CONVENTIONS', 'convention', 'Applies:', or 'Per CONVENTIONS.md'. Grep across all output files returned zero matches. The repo manifest (repo-backend.md) lists a CONVENTIONS.md file in the repository root, but no convention enrichment was performed in any task's Implementation Notes.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture (feature-feature-branch.md) has 'Priority: High'. However, no output file contains 'priority', 'additional_fields', or 'High' in the context of task field inheritance. Grep across all output files for 'priority', 'additional_fields', and 'additional' returned zero matches. No task includes priority propagation.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture has 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 2.0.0' and there is no fixVersion scope config in CLAUDE.md (so it defaults to 'both'). However, no output file contains 'fixVersion', 'RHTPA', or 'additional_fields'. Grep across all output files for these terms returned zero matches. No task includes fixVersion propagation.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: No output file contains a summary comment referencing priority or fixVersion propagation. There is no file that appears to represent a summary comment on the feature issue. Grep for 'priority', 'fixVersion', 'RHTPA', 'High', 'summary comment', 'Step 6c' across all output files returned zero relevant matches.
